mechanisms: Selective competitive inhibition of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT) enzyme activity
Restoration of intracellular NAD+ pools via methyl cycle redirection
Indirect SIRT1 and SIRT3 deacetylase activation through NAD+ substrate availability
Downregulation of adipogenesis transcription factors in white adipose tissue
5-AMINO 1 MQ.safetyNote: For in vitro or animal research only. Not approved for human use. Handle with appropriate PPE. Potential mutagenic or teratogenic effects not fully evaluated. Dispose per institutional biohazard protocols.
5-AMINO 1 MQ.desc: A selective small-molecule inhibitor of nicotinamide N-methyltransferase (NNMT), an enzyme overexpressed in adipose tissue that diverts S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) from methyl cycle metabolism. NNMT inhibition elevates intracellular NAD+ and activates SIRT1/SIRT3 epigenetic regulation.

mechanisms: Selective beta-3 adrenergic receptor stimulation in adipocytes
Lipolytic activity without IGF-1 axis activation
Prevention of diet-induced insulin resistance
Stimulation of fat oxidation via fatty acid mobilization
AOD-9604.safetyNote: Research peptide derived from HGH C-terminus. Does not activate IGF-1 axis. Generally well tolerated; injection site reactions possible. Not approved by FDA for therapeutic use.
AOD-9604.desc: A modified fragment of the C-terminal region of Human Growth Hormone noted for lipolytic stimulation.

mechanisms: Non-hematopoietic erythropoietin receptor (EPOR/βcR) agonism
Peripheral neuroprotection via JAK2/STAT3 signaling
Anti-inflammatory cytokine modulation (TNF-α, IL-6 suppression)
Promotion of small fiber nerve regeneration
ARA-290.safetyNote: Investigational compound for neuropathic pain and inflammation. Well tolerated in Phase II trials. Avoid in active hematological malignancy (erythropoietin receptor activity).
ARA-290.desc: A non-erythropoietic peptide engineered to interact with the innate repair receptor, reducing inflammation and neurodegeneration.

mechanisms: Upregulation of growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts
Nitric oxide (NO) pathway modulation promoting angiogenesis
FAK-paxillin pathway activation for wound healing
Cytoprotective gastric mucosa stabilization via PGE2 synthesis
BPC-157.safetyNote: Research peptide with extensive animal data. No approved human clinical trials completed. Use with caution in patients with history of malignancy due to angiogenic properties.
BPC-157.desc: A pentadecapeptide with potent cytoprotective, angiogenic, and wound-healing properties under experimental conditions.

mechanisms: Upregulation of growth hormone receptor expression in tendon fibroblasts
Nitric oxide (NO) pathway modulation promoting angiogenesis
FAK-paxillin pathway activation for wound healing
Cytoprotective gastric mucosa stabilization via PGE2 synthesis
BPC-157 + TB-500.safetyNote: Research peptide with extensive animal data. No approved human clinical trials completed. Use with caution in patients with history of malignancy due to angiogenic properties.
BPC-157 + TB-500.desc: A pentadecapeptide with potent cytoprotective, angiogenic, and wound-healing properties under experimental conditions.

mechanisms: Bacteriostatic preservation via benzyl alcohol (0.9% w/v)
Isotonic reconstitution medium for lyophilized peptides
BACTERIOSTATIC WATER.safetyNote: Contains 0.9% benzyl alcohol. NOT for use in neonates or premature infants (gasping syndrome). Do not use beyond 28 days after first puncture. For multi-dose vial use only with compatible peptides.
BACTERIOSTATIC WATER.desc: Sterile water containing 0.9% benzyl alcohol. Essential for correctly reconstituting lyophilized peptides for long-term stability.

mechanisms: GHRH receptor agonism with drug affinity complex (DAC) albumin binding
Sustained pulsatile GH secretion from anterior pituitary somatotrophs
IGF-1 axis upregulation for anabolic and lipolytic effects
CJC-1295 WITH DAC.safetyNote: Long-acting GHRH analog. Monitor IGF-1 levels to avoid supraphysiologic GH elevation. Potential for water retention and carpal tunnel at high doses. Weekly dosing protocol; avoid stacking with other GHRH analogs.
CJC-1295 WITH DAC.desc: A long-acting GHRH analog paired with a Drug Affinity Complex to maximize half-life and stimulate continuous GH release.

mechanisms: GHRH receptor agonism without DAC modification
Short-burst GH secretion mimicking physiological pulses
IGF-1 axis stimulation for tissue repair and fat metabolism
CJC-1295 WITHOUT DAC (MODIFIED GRF 1-29).safetyNote: Short-acting GHRH analog. Requires pulsatile dosing (2–3× daily). Monitor IGF-1. Lower risk of supraphysiologic GH accumulation vs. DAC version. Injection site reactions common.
CJC-1295 WITHOUT DAC (MODIFIED GRF 1-29).desc: A synthetic GHRH analog that increases pulsatile growth hormone and IGF-1 secretions without altering cortisol levels.

mechanisms: GHRH receptor agonism without DAC modification
Short-burst GH secretion mimicking physiological pulses
IGF-1 axis stimulation for tissue repair and fat metabolism
CJC-1295 WITHOUT DAC + IPAMORELIN.safetyNote: Short-acting GHRH analog. Requires pulsatile dosing (2–3× daily). Monitor IGF-1. Lower risk of supraphysiologic GH accumulation vs. DAC version. Injection site reactions common.
CJC-1295 WITHOUT DAC + IPAMORELIN.desc: A synthetic GHRH analog that increases pulsatile growth hormone and IGF-1 secretions without altering cortisol levels.

mechanisms: Long-acting amylin receptor agonism (AMY1, AMY2, AMY3)
Central satiety signaling via area postrema and NTS
Synergistic GLP-1 pathway potentiation for weight reduction
Delayed gastric emptying and reduced caloric intake
CAGRILINTIDE.safetyNote: Long-acting amylin analog under clinical investigation (CagriSema trials). Risk of nausea and vomiting especially on dose escalation. Thyroid C-cell monitoring may be warranted. Not approved for commercial use.
CAGRILINTIDE.desc: A long-acting amylin analog investigated for its synergistic action in energy balance and satiation mechanisms.

mechanisms: Cardioprotective gene expression regulation via epigenetic chromatin remodeling
Cardiomyocyte differentiation and repair signaling
Anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 pathway upregulation in cardiac tissue
Reduction of oxidative stress markers in myocardial cells
CARDIOGEN.safetyNote: Peptide bioregulator (Ala-Glu-Asp-Arg) targeting cardiac tissue. Limited peer-reviewed human data. Use under physician supervision. Generally well tolerated in available studies.
CARDIOGEN.desc: A tetrapeptide clinically proven to stimulate cellular proliferation and reduce apoptosis within cardiovascular tissues.

mechanisms: Chondrocyte proliferation and cartilage matrix synthesis promotion
TGF-β mediated collagen II and aggrecan expression upregulation
Anti-inflammatory modulation in synovial tissue
Epigenetic regulation of cartilage-specific gene expression
CARTALAX.safetyNote: Peptide bioregulator (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) targeting cartilage. Limited human clinical data. Well tolerated in available studies. Use under physician supervision.
CARTALAX.desc: A synthetic dipeptide targeting the normalization of metabolic processes in bone and cartilaginous tissues.

mechanisms: Hypothalamic sleep-wake cycle modulation via delta wave induction
Cortisol and ACTH release normalization under stress
Analgesic activity via endogenous opioid system modulation
Antioxidant and neuroprotective gene expression upregulation
DSIP.safetyNote: Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide. May enhance sedation; avoid concurrent CNS depressants. Research compound with limited human PK data. Avoid operating heavy machinery after administration.
DSIP.desc: Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide, a neuropeptide affecting central neuroendocrine regulations and circadian rhythms.

mechanisms: Telomerase (hTERT) gene expression upregulation in somatic cells
Pineal gland melatonin secretion restoration
Anti-oxidant gene expression enhancement (SOD, catalase)
Cell cycle regulation via p53 pathway modulation
EPITHALON.safetyNote: Telomerase-activating tetrapeptide. Well tolerated in available studies. Theoretical concern with telomerase activation in oncological contexts. Long-term safety data limited.
EPITHALON.desc: A synthetic tetrapeptide proven to upregulate telomerase activity and extend cellular lifespan in in-vitro models.

mechanisms: High-affinity activin and myostatin (GDF-8) binding and neutralization
Skeletal muscle hypertrophy via Smad2/3 signaling suppression
Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) bioavailability regulation
FST-344 (FOLLISTATIN).safetyNote: Myostatin inhibitor. Significant muscle hypertrophy potential. Long-term cardiovascular and oncological effects unknown. Strict medical supervision required. Not approved for therapeutic use.
FST-344 (FOLLISTATIN).desc: A potent autocrine glycoprotein that acts as an antagonist to myostatin, substantially accelerating muscle hypertrophy.

mechanisms: Copper-mediated superoxide dismutase (SOD) and ceruloplasmin activation
TGF-β1 upregulation promoting collagen and elastin synthesis
Anti-inflammatory NFκB pathway downregulation
Stem cell chemoattraction and wound healing acceleration
GHK-CU (COPPER PEPTIDE).safetyNote: Topical and injectable copper peptide. Generally well tolerated. Avoid excessive systemic dosing due to copper accumulation risk. Topical formulations may cause transient erythema.
GHK-CU (COPPER PEPTIDE).desc: A naturally occurring copper complex involved in widespread remodeling, collagen stimulation, and anti-oxidant activity.

mechanisms: Ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a) agonism in pituitary somatotrophs
Stimulation of endogenous GH pulse amplitude
Weak ghrelin-like appetite stimulation via hypothalamic NPY/AgRP neurons
Mild cortisol and prolactin co-secretion (GHS-R1a off-target)
GHRP-2.safetyNote: GH secretagogue. May transiently elevate cortisol and prolactin. Monitor blood glucose in diabetics. Avoid in active malignancy. Appetite stimulation is a common side effect.
GHRP-2.desc: A hexapeptide acting as a strong growth hormone secretagogue and ghrelin receptor agonist.

mechanisms: Dermal collagen and elastin synthesis stimulation via TGF-β1
Copper-dependent SOD activation reducing oxidative skin damage
Epidermal stem cell chemoattraction for wound healing
NFκB anti-inflammatory signaling reduction in dermal fibroblasts
GLOW (BPC-157/TB-500/GHK-CU).safetyNote: Combination regenerative peptide blend. Cumulative contraindications of component peptides apply. Angiogenic properties warrant caution in oncological history. Use under physician guidance.
GLOW (BPC-157/TB-500/GHK-CU).desc: A proprietary experimental blend combining cytoprotective properties, angiogenic stimulation, and tissue remodeling mechanisms.

mechanisms: PPARδ (peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor delta) selective agonism
Fatty acid oxidation gene upregulation (CPT1, ABCA1) in skeletal muscle
Mitochondrial biogenesis enhancement increasing slow-twitch fiber endurance
HDL cholesterol elevation and triglyceride reduction via ABCA1 induction
GW-501516.safetyNote: PPAR-delta agonist (SARMs-adjacent). IMPORTANT: Preclinical studies showed rapid cancer progression across multiple species. Currently not recommended for human use by most regulatory bodies. Extreme caution required.
GW-501516.desc: A PPARδ receptor agonist evaluating alterations in fatty acid oxidation and exercise endurance mechanisms.

mechanisms: LH receptor (LHCGR) agonism in Leydig cells stimulating testosterone synthesis
FSH receptor cross-reactivity at high doses
Corpus luteum maintenance via progesterone induction
Spermatogenesis support via intratesticular testosterone elevation
HCG.safetyNote: Gonadotropin hormone. Risk of ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome (OHSS) in women. May promote benign and malignant androgen-sensitive conditions. Monitor testosterone and estradiol levels.
HCG.desc: Human Chorionic Gonadotropin; extensively utilized to stimulate the Leydig cells in the synthesis of endogenous testosterone.

mechanisms: Direct GH receptor (GHR) activation triggering JAK2/STAT5b signaling
Hepatic IGF-1 synthesis induction for systemic anabolic effects
Lipolysis via hormone-sensitive lipase (HSL) upregulation
Protein synthesis enhancement via mTOR pathway activation
HGH.safetyNote: Recombinant human growth hormone. Requires prescription. Risk of insulin resistance, edema, carpal tunnel, and intracranial hypertension. Monitor IGF-1, glucose, and thyroid function regularly.
HGH.desc: Recombinant Human Growth Hormone essential for systemic cellular regeneration, growth, and metabolic regulation.

mechanisms: FSH receptor (FSHR) agonism stimulating follicular development and spermatogenesis
LH receptor (LHCGR) agonism triggering ovulation and testosterone synthesis
Synergistic gonadal stimulation for fertility protocols
Hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis downstream activation
HMG.safetyNote: Human menopausal gonadotropin. Risk of OHSS and multiple gestation in fertility protocols. Requires careful monitoring with ultrasound and hormonal assays.
HMG.desc: Human Menopausal Gonadotropin used in advanced fertility research to stimulate follicular growth and spermatogenesis.

mechanisms: Potent GHS-R1a agonism producing strong GH pulse amplification
Cardioprotective action via CD36/ghrelin receptor in cardiomyocytes
Modest cortisol and prolactin co-secretion at high doses
IGF-1 axis activation for muscle hypertrophy and fat loss
HEXARELIN.safetyNote: Potent GH secretagogue with cardiac GHS-R1b activity. May cause significant cortisol and prolactin elevation. Tachyphylaxis observed with continuous use. Desensitization risk higher than ipamorelin.
HEXARELIN.desc: One of the most potent fast-acting GH secretagogues.

mechanisms: Insulin-like growth factor receptor 1 (IGF-1R) agonism driving PI3K/AKT/mTOR anabolic cascade
Skeletal muscle satellite cell proliferation and differentiation
Anti-apoptotic signaling via Bcl-2/Bcl-xL upregulation
Reduced IGFBP binding compared to native IGF-1 extending bioactivity
IGF-1 LR3.safetyNote: Long-acting IGF-1 analog. Significant hypoglycemia risk — administer post-meal. Promotes cell proliferation; avoid in oncological history. Requires careful dosing titration.
IGF-1 LR3.desc: A highly engineered variant of Insulin-like Growth Factor-1 with an extended half-life dictating profound cellular hyperplasia.

mechanisms: Highly selective GHS-R1a agonism without cortisol or prolactin co-secretion
Pulsatile GH release from anterior pituitary somatotrophs
IGF-1 axis stimulation for muscle anabolism and fat metabolism
No desensitization of GH axis at therapeutic doses
IPAMORELIN.safetyNote: Highly selective GH secretagogue. Minimal cortisol/prolactin co-secretion. Well tolerated for long-term cyclic use. Monitor IGF-1 periodically. Preferred GHS for safety profile.
IPAMORELIN.desc: A highly selective and potent GHRP that stimulates massive growth hormone release safely without spiking cortisol or prolactin.

mechanisms: MC1R-independent anti-inflammatory NFκB suppression in skin
IL-1β and TNF-α downregulation in dermal keratinocytes
Skin barrier integrity restoration
Low-dose anti-inflammatory modulation for sensitive skin applications
KLOW (BPC-157/TB-500/GHK-CU/KPV).safetyNote: Multi-peptide anti-inflammatory and regenerative blend. Cumulative contraindications of component peptides apply. Angiogenic properties warrant caution in oncological history.
KLOW (BPC-157/TB-500/GHK-CU/KPV).desc: A multifaceted blend incorporating anti-inflammatory KPV with tissue repair agents for comprehensive cellular research.

mechanisms: MC1R-independent anti-inflammatory signaling via NFκB suppression
IL-1β, TNF-α and IL-6 cytokine downregulation
Intestinal epithelial barrier integrity restoration
Melanocortin receptor partial agonism
KPV.safetyNote: Alpha-MSH-derived anti-inflammatory tripeptide. Well tolerated. Limited human PK data. May modulate immune response; use caution in autoimmune protocols with concurrent immunosuppressants.
KPV.desc: A tripeptide (Lys-Pro-Val) featuring strong antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

mechanisms: GPR54 (KISS1R) agonism stimulating GnRH pulsatile release from hypothalamus
LH and FSH downstream secretion triggering gonadal steroid production
Reproductive axis gating: puberty onset and fertility maintenance
Tumor metastasis suppression via KISS1 gene pathway (metastin activity)
KISSPEPTIN-10.safetyNote: HPG axis activator. Use in reproductively active individuals requires hormonal monitoring. Potential for premature puberty in pediatric populations. Not approved for therapeutic use.
KISSPEPTIN-10.desc: The active fragment of kisspeptin crucially stimulating the secretion of GnRH to reset the HPTA axis.

mechanisms: Non-peptide GHS-R1a agonist mimicking ghrelin action
Sustained 24h GH and IGF-1 elevation via oral dosing
REM sleep architecture enhancement via GH pulse amplification
Appetite stimulation via hypothalamic NPY/AgRP pathway activation
MK-677 (IBUTAMOREN).safetyNote: Oral GH secretagogue. May cause significant fluid retention, increased appetite, and insulin resistance. Monitor fasting glucose and IGF-1. Not approved for therapeutic use.
MK-677 (IBUTAMOREN).desc: An orally active, non-peptide secretagogue of the ghrelin receptor elevating GH and IGF-1 secretion.

mechanisms: AMPK pathway activation improving glucose utilization
Mitochondrial biogenesis enhancement via PGC-1α upregulation
Retrograde mitochondria-to-nucleus signaling modulation
Folate cycle and de novo purine synthesis regulation
MOTS-C.safetyNote: Mitochondrial-derived peptide. Limited human safety data. Generally well tolerated in early research. Exercise-mimetic effects may potentiate hypoglycemia in diabetics on insulin.
MOTS-C.desc: A mitochondrial-derived peptide regulating metabolic homeostasis and preventing diet-induced obesity in models.

mechanisms: Non-selective melanocortin receptor (MC1R–MC5R) agonism
MC1R activation increasing eumelanin synthesis for UV-independent skin pigmentation
MC4R hypothalamic activation inducing sexual arousal and penile erection
Appetite suppression and lipolysis via MC3R/MC4R signaling
MT2 (MELANOTAN II).safetyNote: Melanotan II. Non-selective melanocortin agonist. Risk of nausea, spontaneous erections, and blood pressure changes. IMPORTANT: Associated with melanocyte activation — monitor nevi. Not FDA approved.
MT2 (MELANOTAN II).desc: A synthetic analog of alpha-MSH inducing remarkable melanogenesis and demonstrating robust aphrodisiac properties.

mechanisms: Sirtuin (SIRT1–7) deacetylase cosubstrate supporting epigenetic regulation
PARP-1 DNA repair pathway cosubstrate
Mitochondrial electron transport chain Complex I substrate
CD38/CD157 ectonucleotidase substrate for calcium signaling
NAD+.safetyNote: IV/SC NAD+ infusion. IV administration may cause flushing, chest tightness, and nausea if infused too rapidly — must be given slowly. Research supports safety at therapeutic doses.
NAD+.desc: A crucial cellular coenzyme found in all living cells, essential in metabolic red-ox reactions and sirtuin function.

mechanisms: NAD+ biosynthesis precursor via Preiss-Handler salvage pathway
Sirtuin (SIRT1/SIRT3) deacetylase activation supporting epigenetic anti-aging
PARP-1 DNA repair system cofactor replenishment
Mitochondrial Complex I substrate restoration improving cellular energy metabolism
NMN.safetyNote: NAD+ precursor. Generally well tolerated orally. Theoretical concern about NAD+-dependent cancer cell proliferation. Limited long-term human safety data.
NMN.desc: A direct precursor to NAD+, actively investigated for its capability to mitigate age-associated physiological decline.

mechanisms: Oxytocin receptor (OXTR) agonism in limbic system modulating social bonding and trust
HPA axis cortisol blunting reducing anxiety and fear response
Uterine smooth muscle contraction stimulation (parturition/lactation)
Central dopaminergic reward pathway modulation enhancing prosocial behavior
OXYTOCIN ACETATE.safetyNote: Synthetic oxytocin. Intranasal use for social cognition research. Avoid in cardiovascular disease. May cause uterine contractions. Psychiatric effects (anxiety, social behavior changes) reported with chronic use.
OXYTOCIN ACETATE.desc: A neurohypophysial hormone mediating complex social, reproductive, and stress-regulating behaviors.

mechanisms: TREK-1 (K2P potassium channel) selective inhibition modulating neuronal excitability
Rapid-onset antidepressant effect via serotonin pathway enhancement
Hippocampal neurogenesis promotion
Anxiolytic signaling via HPA axis normalization
PE-22 28.safetyNote: BDNF-mimetic spadin analog targeting TREK-1 channels. Research compound with antidepressant-like activity in preclinical models. Limited human data. Use under psychiatric supervision.
PE-22 28.desc: A TREK-1 potassium channel antagonist under study for robust antidepressant-like mechanisms.

mechanisms: IGF-1 receptor (IGF-1R) independent signaling via MGF E-peptide domain
Satellite cell (muscle stem cell) activation and proliferation
PEGylation extends circulatory half-life ~144x vs native MGF
Post-exercise muscle repair and hypertrophic signaling
PEG MGF.safetyNote: PEGylated mechano growth factor. Promotes local muscle repair and satellite cell activation. Long-term PEGylation immune response risk. Not for use in active cancer.
PEG MGF.desc: Pegylated Mechano Growth Factor, a slice variant of IGF-1 promoting localized stem cell proliferation strictly in muscle tissue.

mechanisms: Selective cancer cell membrane disruption via HDM-2 transmembrane receptor binding
p53-independent tumor cell apoptosis induction
Non-toxic to normal cells lacking transmembrane HDM-2 expression
Pore-forming necrotic mechanism in MDM2-overexpressing tumor cells
PNC-27.safetyNote: p53-derived cancer-selective peptide. Research compound. Intended for oncological research contexts. Limited human safety data available.
PNC-27.desc: An investigative anti-cancer peptide binding strongly to MDM2 in cellular membranes orchestrating rapid tumor cell necrosis.

mechanisms: Melanocortin MC4R agonism in hypothalamic nuclei mediating sexual arousal
Dopaminergic pathway modulation in mesolimbic reward system
Peripheral vasodilatation via MC3R activation
FDA-approved (Vyleesi) for hypoactive sexual desire disorder in premenopausal women
PT-141 (BREMELANOTIDE).safetyNote: FDA-approved (Vyleesi) for HSDD in premenopausal women. Risk of nausea (most common), transient hypertension, and hyperpigmentation with chronic use. Do not use within 8h of another dose.
PT-141 (BREMELANOTIDE).desc: A cyclic heptapeptide functioning as an agonist at melanocortin receptors heavily researched for sexual dysfunction.

mechanisms: Melatonin synthesis enhancement in pinealocytes
Antioxidant neuroprotection reducing oxidative stress
Circadian rhythm modulation via pineal-hypothalamic axis
Anti-apoptotic effects in neuronal tissue
PINEALON.safetyNote: Generally well tolerated in research models. Limited human clinical data. Not FDA-approved.
PINEALON.desc: Tetrapeptide bioregulator derived from pineal gland tissue. Enhances melatonin synthesis, supports circadian rhythm regulation, and exerts neuroprotective effects via antioxidant activity.

mechanisms: Precision subcutaneous peptide delivery device
31G ultra-fine needle minimizing injection site discomfort
0.3 mL volume suitable for research peptide reconstitution doses
PRECISION INSULIN SYRINGES.safetyNote: Single-use sterile medical device. Never share needles. Dispose in approved sharps container. Verify insulin concentration (U-100 vs. U-40) before use to prevent dosing errors.
PRECISION INSULIN SYRINGES.desc: 31G x 8mm sterile U-100 syringes tailored for painless, extremely precise subcutaneous dosing under research conditions.

mechanisms: Prostate epithelial cell gene expression bioregulation via chromatin remodeling
Anti-inflammatory modulation in prostate stromal tissue
Cytoprotective signaling reducing oxidative stress in glandular cells
Hormonal balance support in androgen-sensitive prostate tissue
PROSTAMAX.safetyNote: Prostatic peptide bioregulator (Lys-Glu-Asp-Pro). Limited peer-reviewed human data. Use under urological physician supervision. Well tolerated in available studies.
PROSTAMAX.desc: A specialized bioregulator peptide targeting cellular regeneration precisely within prostate gland tissues.

mechanisms: Selective triple agonism of GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon receptors (triagonist)
Superior weight reduction vs. dual agonists via complementary metabolic pathways
Hepatic glucagon receptor activation reducing liver fat
GLP-1 mediated incretin effect improving insulin secretion and satiety
RETATRUTIDE.safetyNote: Investigational triple agonist (Phase III). Most potent weight reduction compound in trials to date (~24% body weight). Nausea/vomiting significant on dose escalation. Thyroid C-cell monitoring warranted.
RETATRUTIDE.desc: A novel triple hormone receptor agonist (GIP, GLP-1, and Glucagon) exhibiting pronounced metabolic regulatory effects.

mechanisms: Pan-ERR (estrogen-related receptor α/β/γ) selective agonism
Mitochondrial biogenesis and fatty acid oxidation gene transcription upregulation
Exercise-mimetic metabolic effects improving endurance capacity
PGC-1α co-activation driving oxidative phosphorylation gene expression
SLU PP-332.safetyNote: ERR agonist — exercise-mimetic research compound. Preclinical data only. Limited human safety data. Not approved for any therapeutic indication.
SLU PP-332.desc: An estrogen-related receptor (ERR) agonist simulating structural metabolic adaptations similar to endurance training.

mechanisms: Selective mitochondrial inner membrane targeting
ROS scavenging via dimethyltyrosine antioxidant moiety
Cardiolipin stabilization restoring cristae architecture
ATP synthase efficiency restoration
SS-31.safetyNote: Favorable safety profile in Phase I/II human trials for heart failure and renal disease. Injection site reactions possible. Not FDA-approved for off-label use.
SS-31.desc: Mitochondria-targeted tetrapeptide that reduces ROS generation, stabilizes mitochondrial cristae, and restores ATP synthesis. Powerful tool for mitochondrial dysfunction and oxidative stress.

mechanisms: Tuftsin receptor modulation enhancing innate immune surveillance
GABAergic neurotransmission potentiation reducing anxiety
BDNF and NGF neurotrophin upregulation
Enkephalinase inhibition extending endogenous enkephalin activity
SELANK.safetyNote: Anxiolytic tuftsin analog. Non-addictive with favorable safety profile. May potentiate sedatives. Intranasal preferred. Limited head-to-head data vs. conventional anxiolytics.
SELANK.desc: A synthetic heptapeptide derived from human tuftsin showcasing marked anxiolytic and nootropic potentials.

mechanisms: Selective long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonism (94% sequence homology to native GLP-1)
Glucose-dependent insulin secretion enhancement from pancreatic beta cells
Glucagon suppression during hyperglycemia
Central appetite suppression via hypothalamic GLP-1 receptors and vagal afferents
SEMAGLUTIDE.safetyNote: GLP-1 agonist (Ozempic/Wegovy). Risk of nausea, pancreatitis, and gallbladder disease. Thyroid C-cell hyperplasia in rodents — monitor in MTC risk patients. Requires gradual dose titration.
SEMAGLUTIDE.desc: A selective GLP-1 receptor agonist with structural modifications to extend plasma half-life via albumin binding.

mechanisms: BDNF and NGF neurotrophin upregulation in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus
Dopaminergic and serotonergic neurotransmitter system modulation
Cholinergic pathway enhancement improving memory consolidation
Neuroprotective anti-inflammatory signaling via NFκB suppression
SEMAX.safetyNote: ACTH(4-7)PGP analog. Nootropic and neuroprotective peptide. Generally well tolerated intranasally. May cause transient nasal irritation. Limited long-term human data.
SEMAX.desc: An ACTH analog known for its neuroprotective, neuromodulatory and potent cognitive enhancement effects.

mechanisms: GHRH receptor (GHRHR) agonism stimulating pulsatile somatotroph GH release
Physiological GH axis stimulation without receptor downregulation at therapeutic doses
IGF-1 hepatic synthesis induction
Hypothalamic-pituitary feedback loop preservation
SERMORELIN.safetyNote: GHRH analog. Physiological GH stimulation with lower risk vs. exogenous GH. Monitor IGF-1 levels. Injection site reactions common. Headache and flushing reported.
SERMORELIN.desc: A 29-amino acid polypeptide representing the shortest functional fragment of endogenous GHRH.

mechanisms: SNAP-25 protein competitive inhibition attenuating neuromuscular junction signaling
Reduction of acetylcholine vesicle release at facial neuromuscular synapses
Muscle contraction-induced wrinkle depth reduction (Botox-like topical effect)
Collagen type I synthesis stimulation in dermal fibroblasts
SNAP-8.safetyNote: Acetyl octapeptide-3. Topical anti-wrinkle peptide (Argireline analog). For cosmetic/topical use only. Systemic injection not indicated. Generally well tolerated topically.
SNAP-8.desc: An octapeptide acting as an elongation of Argireline, interfering with the SNARE complex to minimize topical wrinkle formation.

mechanisms: G-actin sequestration promoting cell migration and wound healing
Angiogenesis stimulation via VEGF upregulation
Anti-inflammatory via NF-κB pathway downregulation
Stem cell mobilization from bone marrow
TB-500.safetyNote: Well tolerated in research. Minimal adverse effects reported. Theoretical cancer growth concern at high doses in active malignancy. Not FDA-approved.
TB-500.desc: Standalone TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4 synthetic fragment) vial for tissue repair, angiogenesis, and anti-inflammatory action. Distinct from combination products.

mechanisms: G-actin sequestration regulating actin cytoskeleton dynamics and cell migration
Angiogenesis promotion via endothelial VEGF-A and FGF upregulation
Anti-inflammatory modulation via NFκB and cyclooxygenase-2 suppression
Cardiomyocyte regeneration and cardiac stem cell activation
TB-500 (THYMOSIN Β4).safetyNote: Actin-sequestering peptide with angiogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. Animal studies generally safe. Monitor in patients with oncological history due to angiogenic activity. Well tolerated in human case reports.
TB-500 (THYMOSIN Β4).desc: A synthetic version of the naturally occurring peptide present in all cellular components promoting endothelial cell differentiation and angiogenesis.

mechanisms: GHRH receptor agonism stimulating pituitary GH release
Selective visceral adipose tissue reduction
IGF-1 elevation via hepatic GH signaling
Maintenance of pulsatile GH secretion pattern
TESAMORELIN.safetyNote: FDA-approved (Egrifta). Common adverse effects: injection site reactions, fluid retention, arthralgias. Monitor IGF-1 levels. Contraindicated in active malignancy.
TESAMORELIN.desc: Synthetic GHRH analogue that stimulates growth hormone secretion with selectivity for visceral fat reduction. FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy; used off-label for body composition optimization.

mechanisms: Leydig cell gene expression bioregulation supporting testosterone biosynthesis
Sertoli cell function modulation supporting spermatogenesis
Testicular tissue-specific chromatin remodeling via epigenetic peptide bioregulator mechanism
HPG axis support at peripheral gonadal level
TESTAGEN.safetyNote: Testicular peptide bioregulator (Lys-Asp-Glu). Limited human clinical data. Use under physician supervision. Well tolerated in available studies.
TESTAGEN.desc: A synthesized targeted peptide designed to correct morphological and functional parameters of the male reproductive system.

mechanisms: Thymic peptide bioregulator enhancing T-lymphocyte differentiation
IL-2 and interferon-γ immunomodulatory cytokine upregulation
CD4+/CD8+ T-cell ratio normalization in immunocompromised states
Chromatin remodeling in thymocyte gene expression for immune maturation
THYMAGEN.safetyNote: Thymic peptide bioregulator. Immune-modulating peptide. Use with caution in autoimmune conditions. Limited peer-reviewed data.
THYMAGEN.desc: A dipeptide immuno-modulator aimed at restoring optimal physiological ratios among T-lymphocyte sub-populations.

mechanisms: T-lymphocyte maturation and differentiation in thymus
Enhancement of NK cell cytotoxic activity
Interferon-α and IL-2 secretion stimulation
TLR9 agonist activity amplifying innate immune response
THYMOSIN ALPHA-1.safetyNote: Approved in 35+ countries (Zadaxin). Excellent safety profile. Injection site reactions possible. Generally safe for chronic use under physician supervision.
THYMOSIN ALPHA-1.desc: 28-amino acid peptide from thymic tissue that promotes T-cell maturation and differentiation, enhances innate and adaptive immune function, and exhibits antiviral properties.

mechanisms: TLR-2 and TLR-9 agonism activating innate antiviral and antifungal immunity
Dendritic cell maturation and antigen presentation enhancement
NK cell cytotoxic activity upregulation for tumor immunosurveillance
Th1 cytokine (IFN-γ, IL-2) polarization and regulatory T-cell balance modulation
THYMOSIN ALPHA 1.safetyNote: FDA-approved in some countries for hepatitis and immune deficiency. Well-characterized safety profile. May enhance immune response — caution in autoimmune conditions. Generally safe and well tolerated.
THYMOSIN ALPHA 1.desc: An endogenous modifier of immune response directly augmenting the operational efficiency of T-cell-mediated responses.

mechanisms: Zinc-dependent thymic hormone active only when complexed with Zn2+
T-lymphocyte maturation and differentiation from bone marrow precursors
Suppressor and cytotoxic T-cell phenotype induction
Immunosenescence reversal via thymic function restoration in aged subjects
THYMULIN.safetyNote: Thymic nonapeptide requiring zinc co-factor for activity. Immune regulatory peptide. Limited human clinical data. Generally well tolerated.
THYMULIN.desc: A nonapeptide requiring zinc for biological activity, involved fundamentally in T-cell differentiation and maturation.

mechanisms: Selective dual GIP (GIPR) and GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R) agonism (twincretin mechanism)
GIP receptor agonism potentiating insulin sensitivity in skeletal muscle and adipose tissue
GLP-1 receptor agonism providing incretin effect with glucose-dependent insulin secretion
Central hypothalamic appetite suppression via GLP-1R signaling on vagal afferents and area postrema

TIRZEPATIDE.safetyNote: FDA-approved prescription medication (Mounjaro® / Zepbound®). For research use only when obtained outside approved channels. Contraindicated in patients with personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia syndrome type 2 (MEN 2). Boxed warning: thyroid C-cell tumors observed in rodents.
TIRZEPATIDE.desc: A once-weekly synthetic dual GIP and GLP-1 receptor agonist (twincretin) developed by Eli Lilly. FDA-approved for T2DM (Mounjaro) and chronic weight management (Zepbound). Demonstrates superior glycemic and weight outcomes vs. GLP-1 monotherapy in the SURPASS trial series.
